meta.description: Normalized data from two independent meta-evaluations of nine code-review reports on the honor-control power-profile overhaul. All scores, costs, and rankings are extracted directly from the two evaluator reports. Graphs and tables in MODELS_EVAL_SUMMARY.md are reproducible from this file.
data_sources: SOL_WEB_HIGH_MODELS_EVAL.md (meta-evaluation by GPT-5.6-Sol High with web access)
notes: All costs are in USD and were calculated from session token usage and official API pricing.
GLM scorecard uses integer scores 0-10; Sol scorecard uses decimal scores 0-10.
The two evaluators used the same 8 criteria but applied them independently with different standards.
Issue coverage is from the GLM report's consolidated issue tracker (24 issues total: 1 Critical, 6 High, 8 Medium, 9 Low).

The Sol report was revised after initial publication. The revision introduced a severity-weighted coverage methodology (60% of overall score from finding coverage) and a canonical issue set of 18 issues (3 Critical, 7 High, 6 Medium, 2 Low). The Sol scorecard and rankings below reflect the revised version.
The Sol report's claim-by-claim analysis is consistent with GLM's issue matrix except where noted in the disagreements section.

glm.name: GLM-5.2
glm.methodology: Read all nine reports, verified claims against repository code, ran arithmetic reproductions, checked pricing against API databases. Used integer 0-10 scoring.

sol.name: GPT-5.6-Sol High
sol.methodology: Independent audit with web access. Verified claims against code, dependency behavior, kernel source, and systemd semantics. Ran 150 focused tests. Revised scoring methodology: 60% of overall score from severity-weighted finding coverage against a canonical 18-issue set (3 Critical, 7 High, 6 Medium, 2 Low), with factual accuracy and false-positive control as substantial counterweight. Decimal 0-10 scoring.

reports.notes: Only report to understate severity. Missed CAP_SYS_RAWIO, ProtectSystem, honor-tools mismatch, governor substitution, and many other issues.
Good coverage but missed CAP_SYS_RAWIO, ProtectSystem, honor-tools mismatch, and governor substitution. Most expensive report per finding.

Most unique findings (ProtectSystem=strict vs mask, time.sleep blocking, D-Bus reactivation analysis). However, two High-severity deployment findings (PP-003 ProtectSystem, PP-004 D-Bus reactivation) are disputed by Sol and verified to be incorrect. Best value at $1.79.
Concise but incomplete. Missed PPD ownership contradiction (the most important issue). Least expensive report. Correctly identified sandbox timeout as environmental.

Deepest investigation. Uniquely discovered honor-tools API mismatch through runtime testing. Uniquely caught auto-switch retry loop. Most thorough test coverage analysis. Missed CAP_SYS_RAWIO and ProtectSystem.
Solid analysis. Correctly identified all major issues it covered. Missed CAP_SYS_RAWIO, ProtectSystem, and honor-tools mismatch.

Best overall. Uniquely caught CAP_SYS_RAWIO among high-effort reports. Most actionable fix recommendations. Best severity prioritization. Most thorough documentation analysis. Missed ProtectSystem and governor substitution.
Best value-for-money. At $1.29, identified governor substitution and EPP empty CPU set issue that several higher-cost reports missed. Compact and high signal-to-noise.
Unique turbo finding. Correctly identified CAP_SYS_RAWIO. Uniquely caught turbo/max-performance controls not being applied. Fewer total findings than top reports.

scorecard_sol.description: Scores from revised SOL_WEB_HIGH_MODELS_EVAL.md. Decimal 0-10 scale. Revised methodology: 60% of overall score from severity-weighted finding coverage, 20% accuracy/false-positive control, 8% fix quality, 7% architectural understanding, 5% signal/clarity.

rankings.description: Each evaluator's ranking of the 9 reports from 1 (best) to 9 (worst).

issue_coverage.description: From GLM_MODELS_EVAL.md consolidated issue tracker. Values: 'yes' = identified, 'partial' = partially identified, 'no' = missed. The Sol report did not produce a separate matrix but its claim-by-claim analysis is consistent with this matrix.

issues.description: PPD ownership contradiction: service masks PPD but apply still requires ppd_ok=True
MSR PL2 encoding bug: PL2 enable and time-window bits shifted past bit 63
Missing CAP_SYS_RAWIO in service unit: MSR open will fail with EPERM in production
ProtectSystem=strict blocks systemctl mask (DISPUTED - see disagreements)
Delayed rewrite race: stale tasks not cancelled; boolean failures ignored; success before enforcement
No daemon restore on shutdown/uninstall: PPD/intel_lpmd remain masked
Startup daemon cleanup can exceed 10s queue timeout (4x5s systemctl calls)
Performance governor substitution: rewrite_epp leaves powersave for performance profiles
Startup reconciliation compares only PPD label, not actual hardware values
root_path abstraction bypass: new methods use absolute paths
Capability probe insufficiency: only checks powerprofilesctl binary
File descriptor leak in write_rapl_msr: no try/finally; struct.error not caught
_last_applied_power_profile overrides live hardware observations indefinitely
Zero test coverage for all new methods
Honor-tools dependency contract: >=0.1,<0.2 allows incompatible 0.1.0 from PyPI
Documentation mismatch: README claims AMD sysfs-only EPP but gate rejects non-MRA-XXX
time.sleep(0.1) in rewrite_epp blocks command queue worker thread
rewrite_epp returns success with empty CPU set
Auto-switch retry loop: failed applies retried every 2s with no backoff
Turbo/max-performance controls persisted but not applied by honor-tools 0.1.0
Misplaced # -- Fan -- comment in FakeHardware
Redundant local imports of pathlib shadow module-level import
Inconsistent sysfs write helpers
Documentation/CHANGELOG not updated for the overhaul

severity_weighted_coverage.description: From the revised SOL_WEB_HIGH_MODELS_EVAL.md. Sol's canonical issue set contains 18 independently verified issue categories (3 Critical, 7 High, 6 Medium, 2 Low). Maximum coverage is 92 points. Scoring: Critical=10pts, High=6pts, Medium=3pts, Low=1pt. Full credit for complete correct finding; partial credit for directionally correct but incomplete/overstated; no credit for missed/incorrect/non-actionable.

critical: Dependency API incompatibility (honor-tools 0.1.0 constructor rejects turbo_enabled/max_perf_pct)
PPD ownership/apply contradiction (startup masks PPD but apply requires ppd_ok=True)
PL2 MSR encoding defect (PL2 enable and time-window bits shifted past bit 63)
high: Delayed false success (success before convergence, boolean failures ignored)
Persistent daemon masks (no restore on shutdown/uninstall)
Name-only reconciliation (compares PPD label, not hardware state)
Destructive takeover with weak capability gating (DMI match alone triggers masks)
Persistence/apply transaction inconsistency (hardware success + config-write failure)
medium: Stale delayed tasks (not generation-safe, not fully cancelled)
EPP vacuous success/no readback (empty CPU set returns True)
Requested/effective governor mismatch (performance silently becomes powersave)
Turbo/max-performance contract mismatch (controls persisted but not applied)
Timeout/retry budgeting (4x5s daemon calls vs 10s queue timeout; EPP retries)
Privileged-I/O robustness and test-boundary bypass (root_path bypass, fd leak, short I/O)
low: Inaccurate compatibility documentation (README claims AMD sysfs-only EPP but gate rejects)
Missing focused production-path tests (no tests changed in overhaul range)

disagreements.topic: GLM_MAX PP-003: ProtectSystem=strict blocks systemctl mask
disagreements.glm_position: Confirmed as a real High-severity deployment blocker. systemctl mask writes to /etc/systemd/system/ which is read-only under ProtectSystem=strict.
disagreements.sol_position: Incorrect. systemctl mask is a manager D-Bus operation; PID 1 performs the unit-file mutation. The client service's ProtectSystem=strict does not prevent PID 1 from writing.
disagreements.verification: Verified via busctl introspection: MaskUnitFiles is a D-Bus method on org.freedesktop.systemd1.Manager (PID 1). systemctl mask sends a D-Bus request to PID 1, which performs the symlink creation. The service's ProtectSystem=strict does not affect PID 1's filesystem access.
disagreements.combined_conclusion: Sol is correct. GLM_MAX's PP-003 is incorrect. systemctl mask goes through PID 1 via D-Bus, not as a client-side file operation. ProtectSystem=strict does not block it.

disagreements.topic: GLM_MAX PP-004: PPD reactivated by D-Bus service activation when powerprofilesctl set is called
disagreements.glm_position: Confirmed as a real High-severity issue. PPD's D-Bus service file causes systemd to start PPD when powerprofilesctl connects.
disagreements.sol_position: Incorrect. A successfully masked unit cannot be activated. Reactivation is possible only if masking failed, which the code never checks.
disagreements.verification: If masking succeeds (which it does, per D1), PPD is masked and cannot be D-Bus activated. GLM_MAX's PP-004 depends on PP-003 being true (masking failing), but PP-003 is incorrect. Therefore PP-004 is also incorrect as stated.
disagreements.combined_conclusion: Sol is correct. A masked unit cannot be activated. GLM_MAX's PP-004 is incorrect. However, the underlying PPD ownership contradiction (C1) is real and confirmed by both evaluators.

disagreements.glm_position: Ranked 3rd. Most unique findings, best value at $1.79, thorough MSR analysis, concrete test reproductions.
disagreements.sol_position: Ranked 9th (weakest). High apparent finding count but low validated density: only 5 of 16 findings fully confirmed, 2 materially incorrect, 5 low-value or duplicative. Incorrect ProtectSystem and masked-unit reactivation claims could cause maintainers to weaken hardening.
disagreements.verification: The two disputed findings (PP-003, PP-004) are verified to be incorrect (see D1, D2). GLM_MAX did have unique valid findings (time.sleep blocking, D-Bus reactivation analysis as context). However, the incorrect High-severity findings are a significant reliability concern.
disagreements.combined_conclusion: Sol's ranking (9th) is more defensible than GLM's (3rd). Two incorrect High-severity findings that could lead to harmful remediation actions is a serious reliability problem. However, GLM_MAX's unique valid findings (L2 time.sleep, thorough MSR analysis) should not be dismissed. A combined ranking places GLM_MAX in the lower tier (7th-9th), not 3rd.

disagreements.glm_position: Ranked 6th. Fewer total findings (5). Missed ProtectSystem, honor-tools mismatch, daemon lifecycle restore, startup timeout, and several other issues.
disagreements.sol_position: Ranked 7th (revised down from 3rd). Remains concise and generally accurate, and its CAP_SYS_RAWIO finding is valuable, but it has only 4 fully confirmed findings plus 3 partial findings and completely misses the Critical PL2 encoding defect. High signal-to-noise should not compensate for that level of missed severity.
disagreements.verification: TERRA_XHIGH did miss the MSR PL2 encoding bug (H1), which is one of the most important issues. It uniquely caught the turbo/max-performance issue (L5) and correctly identified CAP_SYS_RAWIO (H2). Its findings were all correct (no false positives).
disagreements.combined_conclusion: The revised Sol ranking (7th) is more defensible than the original (3rd). Missing the Critical PL2 encoding defect is a major completeness failure that should not be offset by conciseness. A combined ranking of 6th-7th is most defensible.

disagreements.glm_position: Ranked 9th (weakest). Only report to understate severity. Missed the most issues. Overstated command-queue timeout as release blocker.
disagreements.sol_position: Ranked 8th. Correct on main source bugs, yet incomplete on packaging, dependency, host lifecycle, and observed-state integrity.
disagreements.verification: Both agree 5.5_HIGH is weak. GLM considers it the absolute weakest; Sol considers GLM_MAX weaker due to false positives. The severity understatement is a significant concern.
disagreements.combined_conclusion: 8th-9th is defensible. The disagreement is minor and depends on whether false positives (GLM_MAX) or missed issues (5.5_HIGH) are considered more harmful.

disagreements.topic: GLM_MAX PP-005: ProtectKernelModules=true prevents loading msr module
disagreements.glm_position: Confirmed as High severity. ProtectKernelModules=true prevents modprobe of msr module.
disagreements.sol_position: Partially correct. The unit cannot load modules, but the application never attempts to load msr. If the module/device already exists, this setting is not the blocker. The real blocker is CAP_SYS_RAWIO.
disagreements.verification: Both agree the real blocker is CAP_SYS_RAWIO (which GLM_MAX missed). ProtectKernelModules is a secondary concern that only matters if msr is not pre-loaded.
disagreements.combined_conclusion: Partially correct. Not a primary deployment blocker. Both evaluators agree CAP_SYS_RAWIO is the real issue.

disagreements.topic: GLM_MAX PP-007: time.sleep in rewrite_epp blocks worker thread
disagreements.glm_position: Confirmed as Medium severity. Blocking sleep on the command queue worker thread.
disagreements.sol_position: Low-value / non-issue. The queue is explicitly a blocking-I/O worker. The actionable concern is timeout budgeting and per-CPU retry duration, not that synchronous code sleeps in the synchronous worker.
disagreements.verification: Both identify the same code pattern. GLM frames it as an architecture bug; Sol frames it as a timeout budgeting issue. Sol's framing is more precise, but the underlying concern (cumulative retry duration exceeding queue timeout) is valid.
disagreements.combined_conclusion: The issue is real but GLM's framing is imprecise. The actionable concern is cumulative timeout budget, not the existence of blocking sleep in a blocking worker. Sol's framing is better.

disagreements.topic: GLM_MAX PP-010: _delayed_power_rewrite not coordinated with mutation lock
disagreements.glm_position: Confirmed as Medium severity. Separate from PP-002 (stale task race).
disagreements.sol_position: Low-value / duplicative. Same ordering defect as PP-002. Merely taking the lock does not solve stale intent without a generation check.
disagreements.verification: GLM_MAX split one concurrency issue into two findings (PP-002 and PP-010). Sol correctly identifies this as duplicative.
disagreements.combined_conclusion: Sol is correct. PP-010 is duplicative of PP-002. The core issue is missing generation/cancellation, not missing lock acquisition.

pricing.notes: GPT pricing from OpenAI Standard API. GLM-5.2 pricing from NeuralWatt API. Token counts from Codex session database (~/.codex/state_5.sqlite) for GPT models and opencode database (~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db) for GLM-5.2. LUNA_MAX cost ($2.11) is from a messed-up session per user instruction; other broken max repeat sessions ignored.
